intro.speaker: Maya
intro.text_en: You made it! Welcome to the Astronomy Club. Let me show you around our little observatory.
intro.text_zh: 你来了！欢迎加入天文俱乐部。让我带你认识一下我们的小观测基地。

intro.speaker: Maya
intro.text_en: First, where we are: a ridge in the San Gabriel foothills, 34°N 118°W, 1100 m up - a suburban site, Bortle 5. Los Angeles glows 32 km to the southwest, which is why we face our telescopes north and east.
intro.text_zh: 先说我们在哪：圣加布里埃尔山麓的一道山脊上，北纬 34°、西经 118°，海拔 1100 米——郊区观测点，光污染 Bortle 5 级。洛杉矶在西南 32 公里外发着光，所以我们的望远镜大多朝北和朝东看。

intro.speaker: Maya
intro.text_en: This is the observation room. The Mission Board on the wall lists tonight's task — check it whenever you feel lost.
intro.text_zh: 这里是观测室。墙上的任务公告板写着今晚的任务——迷路的时候随时去看它。

intro.speaker: Maya
intro.text_en: The Parts Cabinet stores every lens, mirror and mount we own. The Assembly Table next to it is where you build telescopes from those parts.
intro.text_zh: 零件柜存放我们所有的透镜、镜子和支架。旁边的组装台就是你把零件装成望远镜的地方。

intro.speaker: Maya
intro.text_en: The Observation Pad in the middle is where we actually look at the sky. The Club Logbook keeps a record of every observation you make.
intro.text_zh: 中间的观测台是我们真正看天空的地方。俱乐部日志会保存你的每一次观测记录。

intro.speaker: Maya
intro.text_en: That terminal on the right is the Stats Terminal — it computes your telescope's magnification, light gathering and field of view from the parts you installed.
intro.text_zh: 右边那台是数据终端——它会根据你安装的零件，计算望远镜的倍率、集光力和视场。

intro.speaker: Maya
intro.text_en: Before touching any telescope, we start with the oldest instrument of all: your own eyes.
intro.text_zh: 在碰任何望远镜之前，我们先用最古老的观测工具：你自己的眼睛。

intro.speaker: Maya
intro.text_en: Tonight's goal: walk to the central Observation Pad and find the Moon with your naked eyes. I'll be right here.
intro.text_zh: 今晚的目标：走到中央观测台，用肉眼找到月亮。我就在这儿。

level_1_before.speaker: Maya
level_1_before.text_en: Let's begin without a telescope. The Moon is bright enough for your eyes.
level_1_before.text_zh: 我们先不用望远镜。月亮足够明亮，肉眼就能看到。

level_1_before.speaker: Maya
level_1_before.text_en: Try to find it, center it, and really look.
level_1_before.text_zh: 试着找到它，把它放到视野中间，认真看一看。

level_1_after.speaker: Maya
level_1_after.text_en: Nice. Your eyes collected light from the Moon and focused it on your retina.
level_1_after.text_zh: 很好。你的眼睛收集了来自月球的光，并把它聚焦在视网膜上。

level_1_after.speaker: Maya
level_1_after.text_en: But did you notice? You could see the Moon, but not much detail.
level_1_after.text_zh: 但你注意到了吗？你能看到月亮，却看不到太多细节。

level_1_after.speaker: Maya
level_1_after.text_en: Fun fact: those dark patches on the Moon are old lava plains, and every little pit you might make out is a crater - a scar from a rock that slammed into it, sometimes billions of years ago.
level_1_after.text_zh: 小知识：月亮上那些暗色的大片区域是古老的熔岩平原，你隐约能看到的每个小坑都是撞击坑——是岩石砸出来的伤疤，有些甚至有几十亿年历史了。

level_1_after.speaker: Maya
level_1_after.text_en: The Moon has no air, so nothing erodes those scars. A crater from four billion years ago still looks fresh today.
level_1_after.text_zh: 月亮没有空气，所以没有什么能磨平这些伤疤。四十亿年前的撞击坑，今天看起来还跟新的一样。

level_1_after.speaker: Maya
level_1_after.text_zh: 这就是望远镜登场的原因。

level_2_before.speaker: Maya
level_2_before.text_en: Some stars are bright enough to find with your eyes. Others are too faint.
level_2_before.text_zh: 有些星星足够亮，肉眼可以找到。另一些就太暗了。

level_2_before.speaker: Maya
level_2_before.text_en: Quick primer: a star is a giant ball of hydrogen gas so massive that its own gravity crushes the core until atoms fuse together and release light. That's what you're about to look for.
level_2_before.text_zh: 先补个小知识：恒星是一大团氢气球，质量大到自身引力把核心压缩到原子互相聚变、释放出光——这就是你等下要找的东西。

level_2_before.speaker: Maya
level_2_before.text_en: Polaris sits toward the north. See if you can catch it.
level_2_before.text_zh: 北极星在北边。看看你能不能找到它。

level_2_after.speaker: Maya
level_2_after.text_en: Your eyes are amazing, but they're small light collectors.
level_2_after.text_zh: 眼睛很了不起，但它能收集的光很有限。

level_2_after.speaker: Maya
level_2_after.text_en: Polaris itself is about 430 light-years away and roughly 2,500 times brighter than our Sun - it only looks modest because it's so far.
level_2_after.text_zh: 北极星本身大约在 430 光年外，亮度是太阳的 2500 倍左右——只是因为太远，看起来才不起眼。

level_2_after.speaker: Maya
level_2_after.text_en: If we want more detail, we need a bigger light collector.
level_2_after.text_zh: 如果想看到更多细节，我们需要更大的集光工具。

level_2_after.speaker: Maya
level_2_after.text_en: The parts cabinet is open. Tomorrow night, we build.
level_2_after.text_zh: 零件柜已经打开了。下一晚，我们动手组装。

level_3_before_assembly.speaker: Maya
level_3_before_assembly.text_en: This is a refractor telescope. The front lens collects light.
level_3_before_assembly.text_zh: 这是一台折射望远镜。前端的物镜收集光线。

level_3_before_assembly.speaker: Maya
level_3_before_assembly.text_en: The eyepiece helps your eye inspect the image. Let's build it together.
level_3_before_assembly.text_zh: 目镜帮助眼睛观察被放大的图像。我们一起把它装起来。

level_3_after.speaker: Maya
level_3_after.text_en: You just built your first telescope.
level_3_after.text_zh: 你刚刚组装了第一台望远镜。

level_3_after.speaker: Maya
level_3_after.text_en: Now look at the Moon again. Same Moon, better tool.
level_3_after.text_zh: 现在再看看月亮。同一个月亮，不同的工具。

level_4_before_focus.speaker: Maya
level_4_before_focus.text_en: The telescope is built, but the image may still be blurry. That doesn't mean it's broken.
level_4_before_focus.text_zh: 望远镜装好了，但图像可能仍然模糊。这不代表它坏了。

level_4_before_focus.speaker: Maya
level_4_before_focus.text_en: Turning the focus knob moves the eyepiece until the image lines up with your eye.
level_4_before_focus.text_zh: 转动调焦旋钮，会移动目镜位置，直到图像和你的眼睛对齐。

level_4_after.speaker: Maya
level_4_after.text_en: There it is. You didn't just look - you adjusted the instrument.
level_4_after.text_zh: 看到了吧。你不只是观看，你调整了仪器。

level_5_before.speaker: Maya
level_5_before.text_en: New observers often chase the highest magnification. It feels powerful - but it has a cost.
level_5_before.text_zh: 新手常常追求最高倍率。听起来很强，但它有代价。

level_5_before.speaker: Maya
level_5_before.text_en: Jupiter is a gas giant - no solid ground, just deep layers of hydrogen and helium. It's about 780 million kilometers away and could swallow more than 1,300 Earths.
level_5_before.text_zh: 木星是一颗气态巨行星——没有固体地面，全是深厚的氢气和氦气层。它离我们大约 7.8 亿公里，体积能装下 1300 多个地球。

level_5_before.speaker: Maya
level_5_before.text_en: Watch for its Great Red Spot if the view is sharp enough - a storm bigger than Earth that has been raging for at least 190 years.
level_5_before.text_zh: 如果画面够清楚，留意它的大红斑——一场比地球还大的风暴，已经刮了至少 190 年。

level_5_after.speaker: Maya
level_5_after.text_en: Power is useful only when the view stays clear.
level_5_after.text_zh: 只有图像还能保持清楚时，倍率才有意义。

level_6_before.speaker: Maya
level_6_before.text_en: Now that your telescope zooms in, finding targets gets harder.
level_6_before.text_zh: 现在你的望远镜能放大了，但找目标反而更难了。

level_6_before.speaker: Maya
level_6_before.text_en: That's why observers use a finder scope.
level_6_before.text_zh: 这就是为什么观测者会使用寻星镜。

level_6_before.speaker: Maya
level_6_before.text_en: Sirius is a proper star, not a planet - fusing hydrogen in its core about 8.6 light-years away, one of our closest stellar neighbors.
level_6_before.text_zh: 天狼星是一颗真正的恒星，不是行星——它在约 8.6 光年外用核心的氢聚变发光，是离我们最近的恒星邻居之一。

level_6_before.speaker: Maya
level_6_before.text_en: It actually has a hidden companion: a white dwarf, the crushed leftover core of a star that already died. Sirius is technically a two-star system.
level_6_before.text_zh: 它其实还有一个隐藏的伴星：一颗白矮星，是另一颗已经死亡的恒星留下的压缩核心。天狼星其实是一个双星系统。

level_6_after.speaker: Maya
level_6_after.text_en: Wide view to find. Narrow view to study.
level_6_after.text_zh: 宽视野用来找目标，窄视野用来看细节。

level_26_before_assembly.speaker: Maya
level_26_before_assembly.text_en: When we pushed the refractor toward very distant, high-contrast targets, you may have noticed colored fringes. A lens bends blue and red light by slightly different amounts, so they do not meet at exactly the same focus.
level_26_before_assembly.text_zh: 当我们用折射镜观测非常遥远、反差强烈的目标时，你可能注意到了彩色边缘。透镜对蓝光和红光的折射程度略有不同，因此它们不能在完全相同的位置聚焦。

level_26_before_assembly.speaker: Maya
level_26_before_assembly.text_en: A Newtonian reflector replaces the objective lens with a curved primary mirror. Reflection sends all visible colors along the same geometric path, removing lens chromatic aberration.
level_26_before_assembly.text_zh: 牛顿反射镜用弯曲主镜代替物镜。反射让各种可见光沿同一条几何光路前进，因此不会产生透镜色差。

level_26_before_assembly.speaker: Maya
level_26_before_assembly.text_en: The primary mirror gathers and focuses the light. A small diagonal secondary redirects it to the side focuser and eyepiece. Study that path, then build it yourself.
level_26_before_assembly.text_zh: 主镜负责集光并聚焦，小型斜副镜再把光转向侧置调焦座和目镜。先看懂这条光路，然后亲手把它组装出来。

level_7_before_assembly.speaker: Maya
level_7_before_assembly.text_en: The nebula you are hunting tonight is faint - and our 60mm lens is running out of light.
level_7_before_assembly.text_zh: 今晚要找的星云很暗——我们的 60mm 物镜快收集不到足够的光了。

level_7_before_assembly.speaker: Maya
level_7_before_assembly.text_en: So we upgrade the front of the telescope: a 100mm objective gathers nearly three times the light. Equip it, rebuild, and aim for Orion.
level_7_before_assembly.text_zh: 所以我们升级望远镜的前端：100mm 物镜能收集接近三倍的光。装备它、重新组装，然后瞄准猎户座。

level_7_after.speaker: Maya
level_7_after.text_en: Mirrors let us build larger light collectors. That matters when targets are faint.
level_7_after.text_zh: 镜子让我们能制造更大的集光工具。观测暗目标时，这非常重要。

level_8_before.speaker: Maya
level_8_before.text_en: Quick refresher: a nebula is a vast cloud of gas and dust - sometimes glowing, sometimes just blocking light behind it. The Orion Nebula is a stellar nursery, about 1,344 light-years away and roughly 24 light-years wide.
level_8_before.text_zh: 先复习一下：星云是一大片气体和尘埃云——有的会发光，有的只是挡住后面的光。猎户座大星云是一座恒星育婴房，大约在 1344 光年外，直径约 24 光年。

level_8_before.speaker: Maya
level_8_before.text_en: The Orion Nebula is not like the Moon. It's faint. You need light, not just magnification.
level_8_before.text_zh: 猎户座大星云不像月亮。它很暗。你需要的是光，而不只是倍率。

level_8_before.speaker: Maya
level_8_before.text_en: New stars are being born inside it right now. That faint purple smudge is a nursery, not a corpse.
level_8_before.text_zh: 新的恒星正在它内部诞生。那团暗淡的紫色光斑是育婴房，不是废墟。

level_8_after.speaker: Maya
level_8_after.text_en: You collected more light, and the nebula started to appear.
level_8_after.text_zh: 你收集了更多光，星云才开始显现。

level_9_before.speaker: Maya
level_9_before.text_en: Andromeda is far beyond our galaxy. It won't look dramatic at first.
level_9_before.text_zh: 仙女座远在银河系之外。它一开始不会显得很震撼。

level_9_before.speaker: Maya
level_9_before.text_en: But finding it means you're becoming an observer.
level_9_before.text_zh: 但能找到它，说明你正在成为真正的观测者。

level_9_before.speaker: Maya
level_9_before.text_en: A galaxy is a whole city of stars held together by gravity - Andromeda alone holds roughly one trillion of them.
level_9_before.text_zh: 星系是靠引力聚在一起的整座恒星之城——仙女座一个星系就有大约一万亿颗恒星。

level_9_before.speaker: Maya
level_9_before.text_en: It's about 2.5 million light-years away, and here's the wild part: it's falling toward us. In about 4 billion years, Andromeda and the Milky Way will merge into one galaxy.
level_9_before.text_zh: 它在大约 250 万光年外，而且有个疯狂的事实：它正朝我们坠落。大约 40 亿年后，仙女座会和银河系合并成一个星系。

level_9_after.speaker: Maya
level_9_after.text_en: Tonight, you joined the club. Now you've earned your place in the logbook.
level_9_after.text_zh: 今晚你加入了俱乐部。现在，你已经值得把名字写进观测日志了。

level_10_before.speaker: Maya
level_10_before.text_en: Same Jupiter, new question: how much power is too much?
level_10_before.text_zh: 还是木星，但问题变了：多高的倍率算太高？

level_10_before.speaker: Maya
level_10_before.text_en: You can see Jupiter now. Push the power higher - and watch what it costs.
level_10_before.text_zh: 你已经能看到木星了。把倍率推高试试——看看代价是什么。

level_10_after.speaker: Maya
level_10_after.text_en: Bigger is not clearer. You proved it yourself tonight.
level_10_after.text_zh: 更大不等于更清楚。今晚你亲手证明了。

level_11_before.speaker: Maya
level_11_before.text_en: Mars again - but tonight it hugs the horizon.
level_11_before.text_zh: 又是火星——但今晚它贴着地平线。

level_11_before.speaker: Maya
level_11_before.text_en: Mars is a rocky planet about half Earth's size, roughly 228 million kilometers from the Sun. That rust color you'll see is iron oxide dust - the whole planet is basically rusting.
level_11_before.text_zh: 火星是一颗岩石行星，大小大约是地球的一半，离太阳约 2.28 亿公里。你会看到的那种铁锈红色，就是氧化铁尘埃——整颗行星基本上都在生锈。

level_11_before.speaker: Maya
level_11_before.text_en: Here's tonight's real lesson though: seeing. When air near the ground is warm and turbulent, it bends starlight randomly, like looking at something through the shimmer above a campfire.
level_11_before.text_zh: 不过今晚真正要学的是'视宁度'。当地面附近的空气温暖、不稳定时，它会随机弯折星光——就像隔着篝火上方的热浪看东西一样。

level_11_before.speaker: Maya
level_11_before.text_en: Low targets near the horizon look through much more of that thick, turbulent air than something overhead. That's why Mars looks mushy tonight - it's not your telescope, it's the sky between you and it.
level_11_before.text_zh: 贴近地平线的低空目标，要穿过厚得多的抖动大气层，比头顶正上方的目标麻烦得多。所以今晚火星糊糊的——问题不在望远镜，而在你和它之间那层空气。

level_11_before.speaker: Maya
level_11_before.text_en: If the image looks mushy, that's the air - not your telescope. Try backing off the magnification.
level_11_before.text_zh: 如果画面糊糊的，那是大气的问题——不是你的望远镜坏了。试着把倍率调低一点。

level_11_after.speaker: Maya
level_11_after.text_en: Low targets swim in thick air. Less power, steadier view.
level_11_after.text_zh: 低空目标泡在厚厚的大气里。倍率低一点，画面稳一点。

level_11_after.speaker: Maya
level_11_after.text_en: Every telescope has a real ceiling on useful magnification once the air itself is unsteady. Pushing past it doesn't show more - it just shows a bigger blur.
level_11_after.text_zh: 一旦空气本身不稳定，任何望远镜的有效倍率都有一个硬上限。硬要超过它，看到的不是更多细节，只是更大一团模糊。

level_11_after.speaker: Maya
level_11_after.text_en: Remember tonight's shimmer next time you see a target low in the sky. The rule doesn't change - only the air does.
level_11_after.text_zh: 下次看到贴近地平线的目标时，想想今晚这种晃动感。规则不会变——变的只是空气。

level_12_before.speaker: Maya
level_12_before.text_en: Back to the Moon - the one target that rewards high power.
level_12_before.text_zh: 回到月亮——唯一奖励高倍率的目标。

level_12_before.speaker: Maya
level_12_before.text_en: The Moon is bright enough to take the power. But every new eyepiece needs a new focus.
level_12_before.text_zh: 月亮够亮，扛得住高倍率。但每换一个目镜，都要重新调焦。

level_12_before.speaker: Maya
level_12_before.text_en: Tonight's air is only average, not poor like Mars's low horizon - you'll still feel a little shimmer, but it won't fight you nearly as hard.
level_12_before.text_zh: 今晚的大气只是一般水平，不像火星贴地平线时那么糟——你还是会感觉到一点晃动，但不会像那晚那么难缠。

level_12_before.speaker: Maya
level_12_before.text_en: Since we're staring at craters tonight: most of them formed when the Moon had no atmosphere to burn up incoming rocks, so even small impactors punched straight through to the surface.
level_12_before.text_zh: 既然今晚要盯着环形山看：它们大多是在月球没有大气层、无法烧毁来袭岩石的情况下形成的，所以哪怕是很小的撞击体也能直接砸到地表。

level_12_before.speaker: Maya
level_12_before.text_en: Push the power up, then re-focus - the Moon can handle it.
level_12_before.text_zh: 把倍率推高，然后重新调焦——月亮扛得住。

level_12_after.speaker: Maya
level_12_after.text_en: Same rule, different target: brightness pays for power.
level_12_after.text_zh: 同一条规则，不同的目标：亮度为倍率买单。

level_12_after.speaker: Maya
level_12_after.text_en: A dim target and average seeing would have punished that same high power tonight. The Moon's brightness is what bought you the detail.
level_12_after.text_zh: 换成暗弱目标，同样这点大气扰动今晚就会惩罚你用高倍率。月亮的亮度才是你能看清细节的原因。

level_12_after.speaker: Maya
level_12_after.text_en: Same aperture as the Moon needs high power - a faint galaxy needs the opposite. Keep that trade in your pocket for later.
level_12_after.text_zh: 月亮这样的目标才配得上高倍率——暗弱的星系恰恰相反。把这条权衡记在心里，以后用得上。

level_13_before.speaker: Maya
level_13_before.text_en: Your call tonight. Pick a target, pick the power. I'm just watching.
level_13_before.text_zh: 今晚你说了算。选目标，选倍率。我只旁观。

level_13_before.speaker: Maya
level_13_before.text_en: Same average air as usual tonight - nothing dramatic, just the normal little shimmer you've gotten used to by now.
level_13_before.text_zh: 今晚还是那种一般水平的空气——没什么特别的，就是你已经习惯的那点小晃动。

level_13_before.speaker: Maya
level_13_before.text_en: No hint circle judgment tonight either, just your own eye and your own plan.
level_13_before.text_zh: 今晚也没有提示圈来评判——只有你自己的眼睛和自己的计划。

level_13_before.speaker: Maya
level_13_before.text_en: Whatever you pick - a planet with real cloud bands, a scarred old moon, or a star burning hydrogen light-years away - the same question applies: what power actually serves this object?
level_13_before.text_zh: 不管你选哪个——有云带的行星、伤痕累累的月球，还是几光年外燃烧氢气的恒星——问题都一样：什么样的倍率才真正适合它？

level_13_after.speaker: Maya
level_13_after.text_en: You chose like an observer, not a beginner. Chapter logged.
level_13_after.text_zh: 你的选择像个观测者，而不是新手了。本章记入日志。

level_13_after.speaker: Maya
level_13_after.text_en: Magnification Mastery is yours. Next up: finding things in the first place.
level_13_after.text_zh: 「倍率掌握」徽章归你了。接下来：学会怎么先找到目标。

level_13_after.speaker: Maya
level_13_after.text_en: Whichever target you picked, the logic held: match the power to what the object can actually support.
level_13_after.text_zh: 不管你选了哪个目标，逻辑都成立：倍率要匹配目标本身能撑住的程度。

level_14_before.speaker: Maya
level_14_before.text_en: Mars a third time - but tonight you navigate by numbers, not by luck.
level_14_before.text_zh: 第三次找火星——但今晚靠数字导航，不靠运气。

level_14_before.speaker: Maya
level_14_before.text_en: No guessing tonight. Two numbers will take you to Mars: azimuth and altitude.
level_14_before.text_zh: 今晚不用猜。两个数字就能带你找到火星：方位角和俯仰角。

level_14_before.speaker: Maya
level_14_before.text_en: Good news, the air is calm tonight - no fighting turbulence this time. Pure navigation practice.
level_14_before.text_zh: 好消息，今晚空气很平静——这次不用跟大气扰动较劲，纯粹练导航。

level_14_before.speaker: Maya
level_14_before.text_en: One more Mars fact while you're at it: it has two tiny, lumpy moons, Phobos and Deimos - probably captured asteroids, not proper moons formed alongside it.
level_14_before.text_zh: 顺便再说个火星冷知识：它有两颗又小又坑坑洼洼的卫星，火卫一和火卫二——很可能是被引力捕获的小行星，而不是和它一起形成的正牌卫星。

level_14_after.speaker: Maya
level_14_after.text_en: Coordinates turned the whole sky into an address book.
level_14_after.text_zh: 坐标把整个天空变成了一本地址簿。

level_14_after.speaker: Maya
level_14_after.text_en: No shimmering air to blame tonight, no luck involved either - just you, the numbers, and a steady hand on the dials.
level_14_after.text_zh: 今晚没有晃动的空气可以怪罪，也没有运气的成分——只有你、数字，还有转动旋钮的稳定的手。

level_14_after.speaker: Maya
level_14_after.text_en: Once you can read Az/Alt, you can find anything the sky offers, calm night or rough one.
level_14_after.text_zh: 一旦你能读懂方位角和俯仰角，天空能给你的一切目标，你都能找到——不管平静还是难缠的夜晚。

level_15_before.speaker: Maya
level_15_before.text_en: A finder fresh out of the box lies a little. Let's teach it to point true.
level_15_before.text_zh: 刚装上的寻星镜会撒点小谎。我们来教它指准。

level_15_before.speaker: Maya
level_15_before.text_en: Center a bright star in the main scope, then nudge the finder until it agrees.
level_15_before.text_zh: 先在主镜里把一颗亮星居中，再微调寻星镜直到它也对准。

level_15_before.speaker: Maya
level_15_before.text_en: Tonight's sky is calm, just average seeing - nothing to fight. I want your full attention on the calibration, not the atmosphere.
level_15_before.text_zh: 今晚的天空很平静，只是一般视宁度——没什么好对付的。我希望你把全部注意力放在校准上，而不是大气上。

level_15_before.speaker: Maya
level_15_before.text_en: A finder that lies by even half a degree can send you hunting the wrong patch of sky at high power.
level_15_before.text_zh: 寻星镜哪怕只偏差半度，在高倍率下都可能让你在错误的天区里瞎找。

level_15_after.speaker: Maya
level_15_after.text_en: Now your wide eye and your narrow eye agree.
level_15_after.text_zh: 现在你的'宽眼睛'和'窄眼睛'终于一致了。

level_15_after.speaker: Maya
level_15_after.text_en: Do this once per session and trust the finder all night. A finder that lies wastes more time than it ever saves.
level_15_after.text_zh: 每次观测前做一次这个校准，剩下的时间就能放心信任寻星镜了。撒谎的寻星镜浪费的时间比它省下的还多。

level_15_after.speaker: Maya
level_15_after.text_en: Skip this step and you'll spend the whole night hunting through the wide finder for something that isn't where it points.
level_15_after.text_zh: 跳过这一步，你会花一整晚在宽视野寻星镜里找一个根本不在它指向位置的目标。

level_16_before.speaker: Maya
level_16_before.text_en: Feeling confident? Try finding it with the main scope alone.
level_16_before.text_zh: 自信满满？那就试试只用主望远镜找它。

level_16_before.speaker: Maya
level_16_before.text_en: Tonight's air is the same calm average as last time - the challenge is entirely about the workflow, not the atmosphere.
level_16_before.text_zh: 今晚的空气还是和上次一样平静——今晚的挑战完全在于流程，不在大气。

level_16_before.speaker: Maya
level_16_before.text_en: Six degrees of sky looks huge when you don't know where you're pointed. Go on, try it solo first.
level_16_before.text_zh: 不知道自己指向哪里时，6 度的天空看起来大得吓人。去吧，先自己试试。

level_16_before.speaker: Maya
level_16_before.text_en: No finder, no hint circle mentioned yet - just you, the 6-degree eyepiece, and whatever confidence you walked in with.
level_16_before.text_zh: 没有寻星镜，也还没提示圈——只有你、6 度的目镜视野，还有你走进来时的那点自信。

level_16_mid.speaker: Maya
level_16_mid.text_en: Lost? Everyone gets lost in six degrees. Wide first, narrow last.
level_16_mid.text_zh: 迷路了吧？在 6 度的视野里谁都会迷路。先宽后窄。

level_16_after.speaker: Maya
level_16_after.text_en: The narrow field shows detail. The wide field shows the way.
level_16_after.text_zh: 窄视野看细节，宽视野认路。

level_16_after.speaker: Maya
level_16_after.text_en: Small extra fact since Jupiter keeps showing up: it has 95 known moons. Through your telescope tonight you might catch a few of the big four - Io, Europa, Ganymede, Callisto - as tiny points lined up beside it.
level_16_after.text_zh: 木星总是反复出现，补个小知识：它已知有 95 颗卫星。今晚透过望远镜，你也许能看到那四颗最大的——木卫一、木卫二、木卫三、木卫四——像几个小光点排在它旁边。

level_16_after.speaker: Maya
level_16_after.text_en: Everyone learns this the hard way once. Better tonight, in practice, than during a real observation you cared about.
level_16_after.text_zh: 这一课大家都得吃一次苦头才能学会。宁愿今晚在练习里吃这个苦头，也不要在你真正在意的观测里。

level_17_before.speaker: Maya
level_17_before.text_en: Tonight I stay by the door. The sky is yours.
level_17_before.text_zh: 今晚我就站在门口。天空交给你了。

level_17_before.speaker: Maya
level_17_before.text_en: New wrinkle tonight: thin cloud is drifting through. Watch the target dim and brighten as patches pass over it.
level_17_before.text_zh: 今晚有个新麻烦：薄云正在飘过天空。你会看到目标随着云块经过而忽暗忽亮。

level_17_before.speaker: Maya
level_17_before.text_en: Waiting for a gap in the clouds is a real observing skill, not a failure. Every astrophotographer spends half their night watching the sky and the other half watching the clouds.
level_17_before.text_zh: 等云隙是真正的观测技能，不是失败。每个天文摄影师有一半时间在看天空，另一半时间在等云散开。

level_17_before.speaker: Maya
level_17_before.text_en: Combine that with average seeing and no hint circle, and tonight is the real final exam for everything Chapter 4 taught you.
level_17_before.text_zh: 再加上今晚只是一般视宁度、又没有提示圈——这就是第四章教你的一切的真正期末考。

level_17_after.speaker: Maya
level_17_after.text_en: You navigated alone. That's the real badge.
level_17_after.text_zh: 你独立完成了导航。这才是真正的徽章。

level_17_after.speaker: Maya
level_17_after.text_en: You also learned to read a gap in the clouds instead of giving up the moment one crossed your target. That patience will matter even more once we start hunting faint deep-sky objects.
level_17_after.text_zh: 你还学会了读懂云隙，而不是一遇到云挡住目标就放弃。等我们开始追踪暗弱的深空天体时，这份耐心会更重要。

level_17_after.speaker: Maya
level_17_after.text_en: Sky Navigator, logged. Next, we chase the faint stuff.
level_17_after.text_zh: 「星空导航」记入日志。接下来，我们要去追暗弱的天体了。

level_18_before.speaker: Maya
level_18_before.text_en: Same nebula as L7 - but tonight we change what 'success' means.
level_18_before.text_zh: 还是那片星云——但今晚我们改变'成功'的定义。

level_18_before.speaker: Maya
level_18_before.text_en: A nebula never gives you sharp edges. Tonight, a faint smudge IS the victory.
level_18_before.text_zh: 星云永远不会给你清晰的边缘。今晚，看到淡淡的一团就是胜利。

level_18_before.speaker: Maya
level_18_before.text_en: You'll notice a thin haze drifting through again, plus the usual average shimmer. Both take a small bite out of an already faint target - expect the glow to look softer than a bright star ever would.
level_18_before.text_zh: 你会再次看到薄雾飘过，加上一贯的那点晃动。这两者都会从本就暗弱的目标身上再咬一口——它看起来会比任何一颗亮星都柔和得多。

level_18_before.speaker: Maya
level_18_before.text_en: That's exactly why we're accepting Fair quality tonight instead of Good. Perfection isn't the goal - honestly seeing a real object is.
level_18_before.text_zh: 这正是我们今晚接受'尚可'画质而不是'良好'的原因。目标不是完美，而是诚实地看到一个真实的天体。

level_18_after.speaker: Maya
level_18_after.text_en: You saw light that traveled 1,300 years. Blurry, and still beautiful.
level_18_after.text_zh: 你看到了跋涉 1300 年的光。虽然模糊，依然动人。

level_18_after.speaker: Maya
level_18_after.text_en: A crisp view isn't always available and that's fine. Knowing a faint, honest glow when you see one is its own skill.
level_18_after.text_zh: 清晰的画面不是总能等到，这没关系。能认出一团暗淡但真实的光，本身就是一种本领。

level_18_after.speaker: Maya
level_18_after.text_en: Compare this to a bright planet and the difference is obvious - nebulae ask for patience and lower expectations, not more power.
level_18_after.text_zh: 拿它和明亮的行星比一比，差别很明显——星云需要的是耐心和降低期待，而不是更高的倍率。

level_19_before.speaker: Maya
level_19_before.text_en: Third visit to Orion - this time as a controlled experiment.
level_19_before.text_zh: 第三次看猎户座星云——这次是一场对照实验。

level_19_before.speaker: Maya
level_19_before.text_en: Same sky, same nebula. Only one thing changes tonight: how much light you collect.
level_19_before.text_zh: 同样的天空，同样的星云。今晚只改变一件事：你收集多少光。

level_19_before.speaker: Maya
level_19_before.text_en: I cleared the clouds out of tonight's sky on purpose - same average seeing as last time, but nothing else confusing the result. A fair test changes one variable at a time.
level_19_before.text_zh: 我特意把今晚的云去掉了——视宁度还是和上次一样的一般水平，但没有别的东西会干扰结果。一次公平的实验，一次只改变一个变量。

level_19_before.speaker: Maya
level_19_before.text_en: Equip the larger objective before you observe - light gathering makes the difference.
level_19_before.text_zh: 观测前先装上更大的物镜——集光能力决定了差别。

level_19_after.speaker: Maya
level_19_after.text_en: Last night a smudge, tonight a shape. That difference is aperture.
level_19_after.text_zh: 昨晚是一团斑，今晚有了形状。这个差别，就叫口径。

level_19_after.speaker: Maya
level_19_after.text_en: Notice we didn't touch magnification or the sky. One bigger lens, one clear improvement. That's how you isolate what actually works.
level_19_after.text_zh: 注意我们没动倍率，也没动天空条件。只换了一片更大的物镜，效果立刻看得见。这就是如何找出真正有效的变量。

level_19_after.speaker: Maya
level_19_after.text_en: File this away: when a target is light-starved, the fix is almost always aperture, not magnification or focus.
level_19_after.text_zh: 记住这条：当目标缺光的时候，解决办法几乎总是加大口径，而不是加倍率或调焦。

level_20_before.speaker: Maya
level_20_before.text_en: Andromeda again - but the enemy tonight is streetlights, not distance.
level_20_before.text_zh: 又是仙女座——但今晚的敌人是路灯，不是距离。

level_20_before.speaker: Maya
level_20_before.text_en: Andromeda is huge - and the city lights are eating it alive.
level_20_before.text_zh: 仙女座星系很大——但城市的灯光正在活活吞掉它。

level_20_before.speaker: Maya
level_20_before.text_en: Think of light pollution like trying to spot fireflies in a room where someone left every lamp on. The fireflies are still there - your eyes just can't pull their faint glow out of all that background light.
level_20_before.text_zh: 光污染就像在一个所有灯都开着的房间里找萤火虫。萤火虫还在那儿——只是你的眼睛没法从这么多背景光里把它们那点微光分辨出来。

level_20_before.speaker: Maya
level_20_before.text_en: Even though Andromeda looks small and faint through the eyepiece, its true size in the sky is about six times wider than the full Moon. Most of it is just too dim to see - light pollution shrinks it down to a small bright core.
level_20_before.text_zh: 虽然透过目镜看仙女座又小又暗，但它在天空中的真实宽度大约是满月的六倍。只是绝大部分区域太暗看不见——光污染把它压缩成了一个小小的明亮核心。

level_20_before.speaker: Maya
level_20_before.text_en: The glow won't get much sharper tonight - the sky itself is the problem, not your gear.
level_20_before.text_zh: 今晚这团光斑不会变得更清晰——问题出在天空本身，不是你的设备。

level_20_after.speaker: Maya
level_20_after.text_en: Nothing was broken tonight. The sky itself was the problem. Log it honestly.
level_20_after.text_zh: 今晚没有任何设备坏掉。问题出在天空本身。如实记录下来。

level_20_after.speaker: Maya
level_20_after.text_en: No amount of aperture fully cures a bright sky. It only buys back some of what the light pollution took.
level_20_after.text_zh: 再大的口径也治不好一个明亮的天空，它只能帮你从光污染手里抢回来一部分。

level_20_after.speaker: Maya
level_20_after.text_en: Keep this in mind for every faint target from now on: check the sky before you blame the gear.
level_20_after.text_zh: 从现在起，遇到任何暗弱目标都记住这条：先检查天空条件，再怀疑设备。

level_21_before.speaker: Maya
level_21_before.text_en: The club drove out past the hills tonight. Same gear. Darker sky. Try again.
level_21_before.text_zh: 今晚俱乐部开车到了山那边。设备没变，天更暗了。再试一次。

level_21_before.speaker: Maya
level_21_before.text_en: Remember the fireflies-in-a-lit-room problem from the city? Tonight we turned off the lamps. Same faint galaxy, but now the background is dark enough for it to stand out.
level_21_before.text_zh: 还记得城里那个'开灯房间找萤火虫'的难题吗？今晚我们把灯都关了。还是那个暗弱的星系，但现在背景够暗，它终于能显出来了。

level_21_before.speaker: Maya
level_21_before.text_en: Give your eyes a few minutes out here too - they get more sensitive in the dark, a trick called dark adaptation. City lights never let that finish; out here, it can.
level_21_before.text_zh: 也让你的眼睛在这儿适应几分钟——在黑暗中它们会变得更敏感，这个技巧叫暗适应。城市的灯光从不让它完成；但在这儿可以。

level_21_before.speaker: Maya
level_21_before.text_en: City sky and dark sky are the same telescope, the same eyepiece, the same target - only the background light changes, and yet everything changes with it.
level_21_before.text_zh: 城市天空和暗夜天空用的是同一台望远镜、同一个目镜、同一个目标——只有背景光变了，可一切都跟着变了。

level_21_after.speaker: Maya
level_21_after.text_en: Your telescope didn't improve overnight - your sky did.
level_21_after.text_zh: 你的望远镜一夜之间没有变好——变好的是你的天空。

level_21_after.speaker: Maya
level_21_after.text_en: Same aperture, same eyepiece, same everything - the only upgrade was leaving the city glow behind. Dark sky is the cheapest telescope upgrade that exists.
level_21_after.text_zh: 同样的口径，同样的目镜，什么都没变——唯一的升级是离开了城市的光。暗夜天空是存在的最便宜的'望远镜升级'。

level_21_after.speaker: Maya
level_21_after.text_en: Deep Sky Observer, logged. Now let's talk about why the sky keeps moving.
level_21_after.text_zh: 「深空观测者」记入日志。接下来，我们聊聊为什么天空一直在转。

level_22_before.speaker: Maya
level_22_before.text_en: Watch closely. The star is running away - no, the Earth is turning under you.
level_22_before.text_zh: 盯紧了。星星在慢慢逃——不，是地球在你脚下转。

level_22_before.speaker: Maya
level_22_before.text_en: Every star you've ever centered has been quietly drifting the whole time. At low magnification you barely notice. Crank up the power and suddenly it's racing across the view.
level_22_before.text_zh: 你以前对准的每一颗星星，其实一直都在悄悄漂移。低倍率下你几乎察觉不到，一旦倍率拉高，它就像在视野里飞奔。

level_22_before.speaker: Maya
level_22_before.text_en: The Earth spins once every 24 hours, so the whole sky appears to sweep past us at about 15 degrees an hour. Your telescope has no idea you want to keep looking at the same star - you have to fight that spin by hand tonight.
level_22_before.text_zh: 地球每 24 小时自转一圈，所以整个天空看起来每小时会掠过我们大约 15 度。望远镜可不知道你想一直盯着同一颗星——今晚你得徒手对抗这种自转。

level_22_before.speaker: Maya
level_22_before.text_en: Tonight's air is just average, nothing extra to fight - all your attention goes to keeping Sirius centered as it drifts.
level_22_before.text_zh: 今晚的空气只是一般水平，没有额外的麻烦——你可以把全部注意力都放在让天狼星保持居中上。

level_22_after.speaker: Maya
level_22_after.text_en: You just fought the planet's spin by hand. Feel the workout?
level_22_after.text_zh: 你刚才在徒手对抗地球自转。感觉到工作量了吧？

level_22_after.speaker: Maya
level_22_after.text_en: This is exactly why long-exposure astrophotography needs a motor to track the sky - a human hand gets tired, a motor doesn't.
level_22_after.text_zh: 这正是为什么长曝光天文摄影需要电机来追踪天空——人手会累，电机不会。

level_22_after.speaker: Maya
level_22_after.text_en: From now on, every drifting target you fight by hand is really just this same lesson, over and over.
level_22_after.text_zh: 从现在起，你每次徒手对抗漂移的目标，其实都是在重复这同一课。

level_23_before.speaker: Maya
level_23_before.text_en: Tonight the tracking mount does the holding - which frees your eyes for something I want you to see on Vega.
level_23_before.text_zh: 今晚由追踪支架负责稳住目标——这样你的眼睛就腾出来了，我要你在织女星上看一样东西。

level_23_before.speaker: Maya
level_23_before.text_en: Focus as carefully as you can, then stare at the star's edge. If you notice anything strange, it is NOT your mistake. Remember it.
level_23_before.text_zh: 尽你所能仔细调焦，然后盯住星的边缘。如果看到什么奇怪的东西，那不是你的失误。记住它。

level_23_after.speaker: Maya
level_23_after.text_en: Red on one side, blue on the other - and no amount of focusing removed it. That fringe is chromatic aberration, born inside the lens itself.
level_23_after.text_zh: 一边红、一边蓝——再怎么调焦都消不掉。那圈彩边叫色差，它诞生在透镜内部。

level_23_after.speaker: Maya
level_23_after.text_en: Finish your training first. But keep tonight's observation in your logbook - we will come back to it.
level_23_after.text_zh: 先完成你的训练。但把今晚的记录留在日志里——我们还会回到这个问题。

level_24_before.speaker: Maya
level_24_before.text_en: One last lesson: deep sky rewards the patient. Stay with her.
level_24_before.text_zh: 最后一课：深空回报有耐心的人。陪着她，别走开。

level_24_before.speaker: Maya
level_24_before.text_en: Every trick you've learned - aperture, dark sky, tracking - comes together tonight.
level_24_before.text_zh: 口径、暗空、追踪——你学过的每一招，今晚都要用上。

level_24_before.speaker: Maya
level_24_before.text_en: The air is calm, just average seeing - I'm not adding a new obstacle tonight. This is a test of everything you already know, not a new lesson.
level_24_before.text_zh: 今晚空气平静，只是一般视宁度——我不会再给你加新的障碍了。这是对你已学一切的考验，不是新课。

level_24_before.speaker: Maya
level_24_before.text_en: Andromeda will drift like every target does, thanks to the Earth's spin. Tonight the hold is strict - drift out of center for too long and the timer counts backward. Full focus, full patience.
level_24_before.text_zh: 仙女座和所有目标一样会漂移，这是地球自转造成的。今晚的居中判定很严格——漂出中心太久，计时会倒退。全神贯注，拿出耐心。

level_24_after.speaker: Maya
level_24_after.text_en: From naked eyes to a tracked deep-sky watch - you finished the whole path. Senior Observer.
level_24_after.text_zh: 从肉眼到追踪深空——整条路你走完了。高级观测员。

level_24_after.speaker: Maya
level_24_after.text_en: You started by looking at the Moon with just your eyes. Look at you now.
level_24_after.text_zh: 你当初只是用肉眼看了看月亮。看看你现在。

level_24_after.speaker: Maya
level_24_after.text_en: Every telescope you built, every focus you turned, every faint smudge you found - it all led here.
level_24_after.text_zh: 你组装的每一台望远镜，转过的每一次调焦，找到的每一团淡斑——都通向了这里。

level_24_after.speaker: Maya
level_24_after.text_en: And that galaxy you just held steady? In about 4 billion years it will merge with our own Milky Way. You're watching two galaxies on a slow collision course, one careful look at a time.
level_24_after.text_zh: 而你刚才稳稳看住的那个星系？大约 40 亿年后，它会和我们的银河系合并。你正在一次次耐心的观测中，见证两个星系的缓慢碰撞。

level_24_after.speaker: Maya
level_24_after.text_en: The club is proud of you, Senior Observer - and the sky doesn't end here. Keep looking up.
level_24_after.text_zh: 俱乐部为你骄傲，高级观测员——但天空的故事不会到此为止。继续抬头看吧。

level_24_after.speaker: Maya
level_24_after.text_en: One more thing, Senior Observer. We have met a problem the refractor cannot easily solve - and there may be another optical answer in the club storeroom.
level_24_after.text_zh: 还有一件事，高级观测员。我们遇到了折射镜无法轻易解决的问题——也许俱乐部储藏室里，还有另一种光学方案。

1.en: Start with your eyes. Every astronomer begins by learning how to see.
1.zh: 先用眼睛。每个天文学家都是从学会观看开始的。

2.en: Bright stars first. Learn what your eyes can and cannot do.
2.zh: 先找亮星。了解眼睛能做什么、不能做什么。

3.en: Time to build. The objective collects, the eyepiece magnifies.
3.zh: 该动手组装了。物镜收集光，目镜放大图像。

4.en: Blurry is not broken. Turn the knob until it's sharp.
4.zh: 模糊不等于坏了。转动旋钮直到清晰。

5.en: More power is not always more detail.
5.zh: 更高倍率不等于更多细节。

6.zh: 宽视野找目标，窄视野看细节。

7.en: Mirrors gather light too - often more of it.
7.zh: 镜子也能收集光，而且往往收集得更多。

8.en: Faint targets need light buckets, not zoom.
8.zh: 暗目标需要接光桶，而不是放大镜。

9.en: One last target. Make the logbook proud.
9.zh: 最后一个目标。让观测日志为你骄傲。

10.en: Same Jupiter, new question: how much power is too much?
10.zh: 还是木星，但问题变了：多高的倍率算太高？

11.en: Mars again - but tonight it hugs the horizon. Low power, steadier view: thick low-altitude air punishes high magnification hardest.
11.zh: 又是火星——但今晚它贴着地平线。倍率低一点，画面稳一点：低空厚重的大气对高倍率的惩罚最狠。

12.en: Back to the Moon - bright enough to take high power, if you refocus first. Average seeing tonight, barely noticeable.
12.zh: 回到月亮——够亮能扛高倍率，但要先重新调焦。今晚只是一般视宁度，几乎感觉不到。

13.en: Your call tonight. Pick a target, pick the power. No hints, just average air.
13.zh: 今晚你说了算。选目标，选倍率。没有提示，只有一般水平的空气。

14.en: Mars a third time - but tonight you navigate by numbers, not by luck. Calm air, pure navigation practice.
14.zh: 第三次找火星——但今晚靠数字导航，不靠运气。空气平静，纯粹练导航。

15.en: Teach the finder scope to point true before you trust it. Calm skies tonight - focus on the calibration.
15.zh: 先教会寻星镜指准，再信任它。今晚天空平静——把注意力放在校准上。

16.en: Tempted to skip the finder? Six degrees is a big place to get lost in.
16.zh: 想跳过寻星镜？6 度视野足够让人迷路。

17.en: No hints tonight. Navigate the whole workflow alone - and learn to wait out a passing cloud.
17.zh: 今晚没有提示。独立完成整套导航流程——顺便学会等云飘过去。

18.en: Same nebula as L7 - but tonight a faint smudge already counts as success. Thin cloud and average seeing both take a bite.
18.zh: 还是那片星云——但今晚看到淡淡的一团就算成功。薄云和一般视宁度都会咬一口亮度。

19.en: Third visit to Orion - this time as a controlled experiment in aperture. Skies are clear tonight on purpose.
19.zh: 第三次看猎户座星云——这次是一场关于口径的对照实验。今晚特意留了晴朗的天空。

20.en: Andromeda again - but the enemy tonight is streetlights, not distance. Light pollution is fireflies in a lit room.
20.zh: 又是仙女座——但今晚的敌人是路灯，不是距离。光污染就像在开灯的房间里找萤火虫。

21.en: Same gear, darker sky. See what a dark sky alone can do - and give your eyes time to adapt.
21.zh: 设备不变，天空更暗。看看纯靠暗天空能带来什么变化——也让眼睛留点时间适应黑暗。

22.en: The Earth keeps turning under you. Keep the star centered as it drifts.
22.zh: 地球一直在你脚下转动。星星漂移时持续把它保持居中。

23.en: Jupiter, fourth time - but tonight the tracking mount does the work. Dial the rate close to 1x.
23.zh: 第四次看木星——但今晚由追踪支架代劳。把速率调到接近 1 倍。

24.en: The final watch. Hold Andromeda steady and finish the whole path.
24.zh: 最后一次观测。稳住仙女座，走完整条路。

25.en: Repeat the Vega observation deliberately: focus, refocus, swap nothing - and confirm the fringe never leaves.
25.zh: 有意重复织女星观测：调焦、再调焦、什么都不换——确认彩边永远不会消失。这是物理，不是失误。

26.en: Build the Newtonian: external supports on the main blueprint, mirrors inside the Optical Tube Assembly, then c
26.zh: 组装牛顿镜：主蓝图装外部支撑，镜筒组件里装镜面，完成准直后对准月球。

27.en: Same star as L23 and L25 - now through mirrors. Compare what the edge of Vega looks like tonight.
27.zh: 和 L23、L25 同一颗星——这次经过的是镜面。比较今晚织女星的边缘。

28.en: A Newtonian focuses by sliding the eyepiece on the side focuser. Find Jupiter and practice the different feel.
28.zh: 牛顿镜靠侧置调焦座移动目镜来合焦。找到木星，练习这种不同的手感。

29.en: A bright star is the harshest collimation test. Align the optical axis carefully before observing Sirius.
29.zh: 亮星是最严苛的准直考题。观测天狼星前，请把光轴校准到位。

30.en: Point the big mirror at the Orion Nebula and see what extra light-gathering really buys.
30.zh: 把大主镜对准猎户座大星云，看看更强的集光能力究竟带来什么。

31.en: Andromeda through the reflector: let your eyes adapt and use averted vision on the faint halo.
31.zh: 用反射镜看仙女座：让眼睛适应黑暗，用侧视法捕捉暗淡的光晕。

32.en: Earth still turns. Keep Jupiter centered - by hand or with the tracking rate - through the reflector.
32.zh: 地球仍在自转。通过反射镜让木星保持居中——用手修正,或调好追踪速率。

33.en: Mars sits low tonight with poor seeing. A big mirror does not cancel the atmosphere - pick your magnification
33.zh: 今晚火星很低，视宁度糟糕。大口径抵消不了大气——明智地选择倍率。

34.en: Betelgeuse through mirrors: a deep orange-red giant with NO false fringe. A refractor would paint its edge blu
34.zh: 用镜面看参宿四：一颗深橙红色的巨星，没有任何虚假彩边。折射镜会给它的边缘涂上红蓝——你的牛顿镜展示的是恒星自己的颜色。

35.en: Final Newtonian exam: collimated optics, steady centering, a clean deep-sky observation. Then Maya opens the n
35.zh: 牛顿镜结业考：准直到位、稳定居中、一次干净的深空观测。然后 Maya 会打开下一个板条箱。

36.en: A Dobsonian is a Newtonian on the simplest possible base. Build it - count how few parts a rocker box needs -
36.zh: 多布森就是放在最简底座上的牛顿镜。把它装起来——数数摇箱需要多少零件——然后用月球开光。

37.en: No motors, no tracking. Nudge the tube in altitude and azimuth to keep Jupiter centered.
37.zh: 没有马达，没有追踪。用手在高度和方位上轻推镜筒，让木星保持居中。

38.en: Longer hold, same hands. Find the push-wait-push rhythm every Dobsonian owner knows.
38.zh: 更长的保持时间，同一双手。找到每个多布森用户都懂的"推-等-推"节奏。

39.en: Observers call big Dobsonians "light buckets". Point yours at the Orion Nebula and see why.
39.zh: 观测者把大多布森叫作"光之水桶"。把它对准猎户座大星云，看看为什么。

40.en: The 30mm eyepiece gives a wide, bright field. Deep sky rarely wants maximum power - frame the whole galaxy ins
40.zh: 30mm 目镜给出又宽又亮的视场。深空很少需要最高倍率——把整个星系框进来。

41.en: Clouds drift through tonight. Watch the gaps, observe when the sky opens, and do not fight the weather.
41.zh: 今晚有云飘过。盯住云缝，天空放开时抓紧观测，不要和天气较劲。

42.en: Mars hugs the horizon. Apply the seeing lesson: drop the power, wait for still moments.
42.zh: 火星贴着地平线。用上视宁度那一课：降低倍率，等待大气平静的瞬间。

43.en: Focus, track by hand, and pick the right eyepiece - all in one session on Jupiter.
43.zh: 调焦、手动追踪、选对目镜——在一次木星观测里全部用上。

44.en: A long, steady hold on Andromeda with nothing but your fingertips. The hardest hands-on test in the club.
44.zh: 只靠指尖，对仙女座进行一次漫长而稳定的凝视。俱乐部里最难的手动考验。

45.en: Big mirror, simple box, your hands - prove you can drive it. Then Maya explains why some tubes fold their ligh
45.zh: 大主镜、简单摇箱、你的双手——证明你能驾驭它。然后 Maya 会解释为什么有些镜筒要折叠光路。

46.en: Build the Cassegrain: a long telescope hidden in a short tube. First light on the Moon through the rear focuse
46.zh: 组装卡塞格林：藏在短镜筒里的长望远镜。通过后置调焦座，用月球开光。

47.en: Long focal length means high magnification. Jupiter looks bigger than ever - if you can find it in the narrow
47.zh: 长焦距意味着高倍率。木星比以往任何时候都大——前提是你能在窄视场里找到它。

48.en: A narrow field punishes sloppy aiming. Use the finder scope discipline you learned long ago.
48.zh: 窄视场惩罚粗糙的瞄准。用上你很久以前学会的寻星镜纪律。

49.en: Mars is tiny. The folded path gives you the scale - the atmosphere decides if you keep it.
49.zh: 火星很小。折叠光路给了你放大倍数——大气决定你能不能保住它。

50.en: High power amplifies every ripple of air. Wait for the still moments; drop power if they never come.
50.zh: 高倍率会放大每一丝大气抖动。等待平静的瞬间；等不到就降低倍率。

51.en: At high magnification the sky drifts FAST across the narrow field. Keep Mars centered.
51.zh: 高倍率下，天空在窄视场里漂移得飞快。让火星保持居中。

52.en: Turn the folded focal length on the Moon: single craters fill the eyepiece.
52.zh: 把折叠焦距对准月球：单个环形山就能填满目镜。

53.en: Same Jupiter, two mirrors: the Newtonian showed a wide bright disk, the Cassegrain a large detailed one. Neith
53.zh: 同一颗木星，两种镜子：牛顿镜给出宽而亮的圆面，卡塞格林给出大而细的圆面。没有谁全面更好。

54.en: Clouds cross a narrow field quickly and completely. Time your observation between them.
54.zh: 云穿过窄视场又快又彻底。在云缝之间安排你的观测。

55.en: Master the folded path under real conditions. Then Maya brings out an even stranger fold - centuries older.
55.zh: 在真实条件下驾驭折叠光路。然后 Maya 拿出一种更奇特的折叠——它还要古老几个世纪。

56.en: Build the Gregorian: light passes through a real focus BEFORE the concave secondary. Alignment must be stricte
56.zh: 组装格里高利镜：光线先穿过一个真实焦点，再到达凹面副镜。对准要求比以往任何一台都严格。

57.en: You observed Sirius with the refractor long ago. Feel where the Gregorian snaps into focus and compare.
57.zh: 很久以前你用折射镜看过天狼星。感受格里高利镜"咔哒"合焦的位置，并做比较。

58.en: The extra focus makes the image upright and high-contrast. Vega, once more, without any fringe.
58.zh: 多出的焦点让成像正立且高对比。再看一次织女星——依然没有任何彩边。

59.en: Small target, strict optics. Every arcsecond of alignment error shows on Mars.
59.zh: 小目标，严苛光学。每一角秒的对准误差都会显现在火星上。

60.en: Study Jupiter's cloud bands: the Gregorian's clean contrast is its quiet strength.
60.zh: 研究木星云带：干净的对比度是格里高利镜低调的强项。

61.en: Can the two-mirror fold handle a faint nebula? Find out on Orion.
61.zh: 双镜折叠能应付暗弱星云吗？在猎户座上找到答案。

62.en: Drift correction through a folded path: keep Sirius centered.
62.zh: 在折叠光路中做漂移修正：让天狼星保持居中。

63.en: Poor seeing on a strict instrument: learn which errors are yours and which are the sky's.
63.zh: 严苛仪器遇上糟糕视宁度：分清哪些误差是你的，哪些是天空的。

64.en: Newtonian, Cassegrain, Gregorian - each fold has a reason. Prove you can explain all three on golden Arcturus.
64.zh: 牛顿、卡塞格林、格里高利——每种折叠都有理由。用金色的大角星证明你能说清这三者。

65.en: The last ground telescope exam. Every mirror you own still fights one enemy: the atmosphere itself.
65.zh: 最后一场地面望远镜考试。你拥有的每一面镜子仍在对抗同一个敌人：大气本身。

66.en: Assemble the segmented space telescope: 18 mirror segments, an infrared instrument package, and a tennis-court
66.zh: 组装分段式空间望远镜：18 块镜片、红外仪器包，和一面网球场大小的遮阳板。

67.en: Infrared is not visible light. Point at Orion and ask a different question: not "what shines" but "what is war
67.zh: 红外不是可见光。对准猎户座，问一个不同的问题：不是"什么在发光"，而是"什么是温热的"。

68.en: Dust blocks visible light but not infrared. Find the newborn stars hiding inside the nebula.
68.zh: 尘埃挡住可见光，却挡不住红外。找到藏在星云内部的新生恒星。

69.en: A warm infrared telescope observes only itself. Manage the sunshield so the instruments stay cold.
69.zh: 发热的红外望远镜只能观测它自己。用好遮阳板，让仪器保持低温。

70.en: Point the telescope AND keep Sun, Earth and Moon behind the shield - both at once.
70.zh: 既要指向目标，又要让太阳、地球和月亮全躲在遮阳板后——两者必须同时成立。

71.en: Andromeda in infrared shows warm dust lanes invisible from the ground. Map them.
71.zh: 红外下的仙女座露出地面看不见的温暖尘埃带。把它们描绘出来。

72.en: Compare tonight's Orion with your Newtonian records: what did the atmosphere steal from you?
72.zh: 把今晚的猎户座和你的牛顿镜记录对比：大气到底偷走了什么？

73.en: Betelgeuse is cool and red - a natural infrared beacon. Measure the giant.
73.zh: 参宿四又冷又红——天生的红外灯塔。测量这颗巨星。

74.en: Deploy, cool, point, observe: run a complete infrared session by yourself.
74.zh: 展开、降温、指向、观测：独立完成一次完整的红外观测。

75.en: You now observe light no eye can see. There is radiation even "colder" than infrared - and for that, we have a
75.zh: 你已能观测眼睛看不见的光。还有比红外"更冷"的辐射——为了它，我们在贵州有一个巨人。

76.en: Set up FAST: active dish, suspended feed cabin, signal processor. There is no eyepiece - there never will be.
76.zh: 搭建 FAST：主动反射面、悬吊馈源舱、信号处理器。这里没有目镜——永远不会有。

77.en: Sweep the frequency band until the pulsar's repeating pulse rises out of the noise.
77.zh: 扫过频段，直到脉冲星重复的脉冲从噪声中浮现。

78.en: Read the trace and measure the period: 0.714 seconds per revolution of a dead star.
78.zh: 读取轨迹并测量周期：一颗死亡恒星每 0.714 秒自转一圈。

79.en: A radio telescope points with signal strength. Move the feed until the pulse peaks - that is your pulsar.
79.zh: 射电望远镜靠信号强度指向。移动馈源直到脉冲最强——那就是你的脉冲星。

80.en: Tonight the pulse barely clears the noise floor. Tune finer, read more carefully.
80.zh: 今晚脉冲勉强高出噪声底。调得更细，读得更仔细。

81.en: Longer listening makes faint pulses add up. Radio astronomy rewards patience with certainty.
81.zh: 听得更久，微弱脉冲就会累加起来。射电天文用确定性回报耐心。

82.en: The feed cabin hangs from cables above the dish. Practice fine positioning against a moving sky.
82.zh: 馈源舱靠缆索悬在反射面上方。练习在移动的天空下做精细定位。

83.en: Interference is radio astronomy's light pollution. Learn to tell the pulsar from human noise.
83.zh: 干扰就是射电天文的光污染。学会把脉冲星和人造噪声区分开。

84.en: Tune, localize, measure, record - one complete radio observation, alone.
84.zh: 调谐、定位、测量、记录——独立完成一次完整的射电观测。

85.en: Data IS the observation - you have proven it. One question remains: what happens when every window opens at on
85.zh: 数据本身就是观测——你已经证明了这一点。只剩一个问题：当所有窗口同时打开，会发生什么？

86.en: One nebula, three observatories. Decide which instrument answers tonight's question.
86.zh: 一片星云，三座观测台。决定哪台仪器能回答今晚的问题。

87.en: Observe Orion in visible light and infrared, and note what each reveals that the other hides.
87.zh: 分别用可见光和红外观测猎户座，记下彼此揭示与隐藏的东西。

88.en: Stars in visible light, dust in infrared, cold gas in radio: three portraits of one galaxy.
88.zh: 可见光里的恒星、红外里的尘埃、射电里的冷气体：同一个星系的三幅肖像。

89.en: Lay the three records side by side. What claim does EVERY window support?
89.zh: 把三份记录并排放好。哪个结论是所有窗口都支持的？

90.en: No instructions this time. Pick the question, pick the instrument, run the observation.
90.zh: 这次没有指令。自己提问题、自己选仪器、自己完成观测。

91.en: Everything you have learned, in one report: one galaxy, every window of light, one supported conclusion.
91.zh: 把你学到的一切写进一份报告：一个星系、所有的光之窗口、一个被证据支持的结论。

level_25_before.speaker: Maya
level_25_before.text_en: Tonight's target is Vega — a brilliant blue-white star. Your refractor skills are solid now, so I want you to do something new: look CLOSELY at the star's edge.
level_25_before.text_zh: 今晚的目标是织女星——一颗明亮的蓝白色恒星。你的折射镜已经用得很熟练了，所以这次我要你做一件新的事：仔细看恒星的边缘。

level_25_before.speaker: Maya
level_25_before.text_en: Focus as carefully as you can. If you see anything strange around the star, don't assume you made a mistake. Just observe it, and remember it.
level_25_before.text_zh: 尽你所能仔细调焦。如果你在恒星周围看到什么奇怪的东西，不要以为是自己操作错了。观察它，记住它。

level_25_after.speaker: Maya
level_25_after.text_en: You saw it: a red fringe on one side, blue on the other. That is chromatic aberration — and it is NOT your fault.
level_25_after.text_zh: 你看到了：一侧偏红、一侧偏蓝的彩色边缘。这叫色差——而且这不是你的错。

level_25_after.speaker: Maya
level_25_after.text_en: A glass lens bends blue light more strongly than red light, so they can never land on exactly the same focal point. No eyepiece swap or focusing can fully fix it.
level_25_after.text_zh: 玻璃透镜对蓝光的偏折比红光更强，所以它们永远无法落在完全相同的焦点上。换目镜、再调焦都不能彻底解决。

level_25_after.speaker: Maya
level_25_after.text_en: We can keep using the refractor — but if we want a cleaner image, we need a telescope that collects light WITHOUT passing it through a big lens. A reflector.
level_25_after.text_zh: 我们可以继续用折射镜——但如果想要更干净的图像，就需要一台不让光穿过大透镜的望远镜。反射式望远镜。

level_25_after.speaker: Maya
level_25_after.text_en: Check the Mission Board, then meet me at the Assembly Table. The Newtonian parts are already in the cabinet.
level_25_after.text_zh: 先看看任务板，然后到组装台找我。牛顿镜的零件已经放进零件柜了。

first_tube_subassembly.speaker: Maya
first_tube_subassembly.text_en: One more thing before you touch that tube: the Optical Tube Assembly is a COMPONENT WITH AN INTERIOR. Click it to open the tube blueprint and install the mirrors inside.
first_tube_subassembly.text_zh: 碰那个镜筒之前再说一件事：光学镜筒是一个"有内部结构的组件"。点击它就能打开镜筒蓝图，在里面安装镜片。

first_tube_subassembly.speaker: Maya
first_tube_subassembly.text_en: Save the tube when it's complete and you'll come right back to this bench, with the tube shown as installed. You can reopen it anytime to check your work.
first_tube_subassembly.text_zh: 装完之后保存镜筒，你会直接回到这个装配台，镜筒会显示为已安装。之后你随时可以再点开它检查。

first_collimation.speaker: Maya
first_collimation.text_en: Before a reflector can observe, its mirrors must agree on ONE optical axis. That's collimation: tilting the primary and secondary until light lands dead center.
first_collimation.text_zh: 反射镜观测之前，它的几面镜子必须对齐在同一条光轴上。这就是准直：微调主镜和副镜，直到光线正中靶心。

first_collimation.speaker: Maya
first_collimation.text_en: A perfectly built Newtonian with bad collimation gives worse images than a cheap refractor. Take your time in there.
first_collimation.text_zh: 一台装配完美但准直糟糕的牛顿镜，成像还不如便宜的折射镜。进去以后慢慢来。

level_27_after.speaker: Maya
level_27_after.text_en: Same star, same sky — but no colored fringe this time. Mirrors reflect every color along the same path.
level_27_after.text_zh: 同一颗星，同一片天空——但这次没有彩色边缘了。镜面把所有颜色的光沿同一条路径反射。

level_27_after.speaker: Maya
level_27_after.text_en: But notice what it cost you: you had to build a tube full of precision mirrors and then collimate them. A reflector is not better at everything — it trades lens color error for alignment work.
level_27_after.text_zh: 但也注意它的代价：你得装好一整筒精密镜片，还要给它们准直。反射镜不是所有方面都更好——它用准直工作换掉了透镜的色差。

level_27_after.speaker: Maya
level_27_after.text_en: Next, let's see what this mirror is REALLY good at: faint, deep-sky targets.
level_27_after.text_zh: 接下来，看看这面镜子真正擅长什么：暗弱的深空目标。

level_30_before.speaker: Maya
level_30_before.text_en: The Orion Nebula — a stellar nursery. With the refractor it was a faint smudge. Your Newtonian's mirror gathers far more light.
level_30_before.text_zh: 猎户座大星云——一片恒星育婴室。用折射镜看它只是一团淡斑。你的牛顿镜主镜能收集多得多的光。

level_30_before.speaker: Maya
level_30_before.text_en: Aperture is everything for deep sky: double the mirror diameter and you collect four times the light. Keep the nebula centered and let your eyes adapt.
level_30_before.text_zh: 对深空来说，口径就是一切：主镜直径翻倍，集光量就是四倍。把星云保持在视野中央，让眼睛慢慢适应黑暗。

level_30_after.speaker: Maya
level_30_after.text_en: Look at that structure — the wings, the bright core. THIS is why we built the reflector.
level_30_after.text_zh: 看看那些结构——延展的云翼、明亮的核心。这就是我们组装反射镜的原因。

level_26_after.speaker: Maya
level_26_after.text_en: First light through mirrors - and the Moon never looked cleaner. Log it: this is what a collimated reflector gives you.
level_26_after.text_zh: 镜面的第一束光——月球从未这么干净过。记下来：这就是一台准直到位的反射镜给你的回报。

level_27_before.speaker: Maya
level_27_before.text_en: Back to Vega - the same star that betrayed the lens in L23. Look at its edge through mirrors this time.
level_27_before.text_zh: 回到织女星——就是在 L23 出卖了透镜的那颗星。这次透过镜面看它的边缘。

level_28_before.speaker: Maya
level_28_before.text_en: Why Jupiter tonight? Because focusing practice needs a target that PUNISHES bad focus - and Jupiter's big banded disk shows every miss instantly.
level_28_before.text_zh: 为什么今晚是木星？因为调焦练习需要一个会惩罚失误的目标——木星又大又满是条纹的圆面，会立刻暴露每一次没对准。

level_28_before.speaker: Maya
level_28_before.text_en: A side focuser feels different: you slide the eyepiece, not the objective. Find the snap point on Jupiter.
level_28_before.text_zh: 侧置调焦座手感不同：你移动的是目镜，不是物镜。在木星上找到合焦点。

level_28_after.speaker: Maya
level_28_after.text_en: Same physics, new hands. Every telescope family will re-teach you focusing in its own accent.
level_28_after.text_zh: 同样的物理，不同的手感。每个望远镜家族都会用自己的口音重新教你调焦。

level_29_before.speaker: Maya
level_29_before.text_en: Tonight I loosened your collimation on purpose. Fix the optical axis before Sirius - a bright star exposes every misalignment.
level_29_before.text_zh: 今晚我故意松动了你的准直。观测天狼星前把光轴校好——亮星会暴露每一点失准。

level_29_after.speaker: Maya
level_29_after.text_en: A perfectly built Newtonian with bad collimation is worse than a cheap refractor. Now you know it in your fingers, not just your notes.
level_29_after.text_zh: 装配完美但准直糟糕的牛顿镜，还不如便宜的折射镜。现在这句话长在你手上了，不只在笔记里。

level_31_before.speaker: Maya
level_31_before.text_en: Andromeda again - but tonight your mirror is bigger than any lens we own. Let your eyes adapt and use averted vision.
level_31_before.text_zh: 又是仙女座——但今晚你的主镜比我们任何一块透镜都大。让眼睛适应黑暗，用侧视法。

level_31_after.speaker: Maya
level_31_after.text_en: The halo reached further than ever. Aperture plus dark eyes - that is the whole recipe for deep sky.
level_31_after.text_zh: 光晕比以往延伸得都远。口径加上适应黑暗的眼睛——这就是深空观测的全部配方。

level_32_before.speaker: Maya
level_32_before.text_en: The Earth has not stopped turning just because your telescope got better. Keep Jupiter centered.
level_32_before.text_zh: 地球不会因为你的望远镜变好了就停止自转。让木星保持居中。

level_32_after.speaker: Maya
level_32_after.text_en: Drift correction is now muscle memory. Good - the next telescope will demand it constantly.
level_32_after.text_zh: 漂移修正已经成了肌肉记忆。很好——下一台望远镜会时时刻刻要求它。

level_33_before.speaker: Maya
level_33_before.text_en: Mars is low and the air is boiling. A big mirror does NOT cancel the atmosphere - choose your magnification like a meteorologist.
level_33_before.text_zh: 火星很低，大气在沸腾。大口径抵消不了大气——像气象学家一样选择你的倍率。

level_33_after.speaker: Maya
level_33_after.text_en: You dropped the power and won the detail. Remember: the atmosphere sets a ceiling no aperture can break.
level_33_after.text_zh: 你降低了倍率，反而赢得了细节。记住：大气设下的天花板，任何口径都砸不穿。

level_91_before.speaker: Maya
level_91_before.text_en: Your final task, Observer: a complete report on Andromeda. Choose your instruments, gather visible, infrared and radio evidence, and draw a conclusion the data supports.
level_91_before.text_zh: 你的最终任务，观测员：一份关于仙女座的完整报告。选择仪器，收集可见光、红外和无线电证据，写下数据支持的结论。

level_91_before.speaker: Maya
level_91_before.text_en: From a naked eye on the Moon to a multi-wavelength report on a galaxy. Show me everything you've learned.
level_91_before.text_zh: 从用肉眼看月亮，到给一个星系写多波段报告。把你学到的一切展示给我看。

level_91_after.speaker: Maya
level_91_after.text_en: Report accepted. Signed, sealed, and entered into the club archive. You came here as a curious kid — you're leaving this program as an Observer.
level_91_after.text_zh: 报告通过。签字、盖章、存入俱乐部档案。你来的时候是个好奇的孩子——完成这个项目时，你已经是一名观测员了。

level_91_after.speaker: Maya
level_91_after.text_en: The sky doesn't end here. It never ends anywhere. Keep looking up.
level_91_after.text_zh: 天空不会在这里结束。它在任何地方都不会结束。继续抬头看吧。

level_90_before.speaker: Maya
level_90_before.text_en: No instructions tonight. Choose the question, choose the instrument, run the session. Surprise me.
level_90_before.text_zh: 今晚没有指令。自己选问题、选仪器、完成观测。让我惊讶一次。

level_90_after.speaker: Maya
level_90_after.text_en: You designed an observation from nothing. That is the whole job, and you just did it.
level_90_after.text_zh: 你从零设计了一次观测。这就是这份工作的全部，而你刚刚完成了它。

level_89_before.speaker: Maya
level_89_before.text_en: Lay the three records side by side. Which claim does EVERY window support?
level_89_before.text_zh: 把三份记录并排放好。哪个结论是所有窗口都支持的？

level_89_after.speaker: Maya
level_89_after.text_en: No single instrument saw the whole galaxy. Together, they did. Modern astronomy is exactly this: stitching one truth out of many kinds of light.
level_89_after.text_zh: 没有一台仪器看到了完整的星系，但它们合在一起做到了。现代天文学正是如此：用多种光拼出同一个真相。

level_88_before.speaker: Maya
level_88_before.text_en: Andromeda across the spectrum. Visible light shows its stars, infrared reveals its warm dust lanes, radio traces its cold hydrogen gas. Same galaxy — three different portraits.
level_88_before.text_zh: 横跨波谱看仙女座。可见光展示它的恒星，红外揭示温暖的尘埃带，无线电描出寒冷的氢气。同一个星系——三幅不同的肖像。

level_88_before.speaker: Maya
level_88_before.text_en: Collect the evidence from each window, then COMPARE. What does each one see that the others miss?
level_88_before.text_zh: 从每个窗口收集证据，然后比较。每一种波段看到了什么其他波段错过的东西？

level_88_after.speaker: Maya
level_88_after.text_en: Three portraits collected. Tomorrow we hang them side by side.
level_88_after.text_zh: 三幅肖像集齐了。明天我们把它们并排挂起来。

level_87_before.speaker: Maya
level_87_before.text_en: Observe Orion twice tonight - visible, then infrared. Write down what each window hides.
level_87_before.text_zh: 今晚观测猎户座两次——先可见光，后红外。写下每个窗口各自隐藏了什么。

level_87_after.speaker: Maya
level_87_after.text_en: Two windows, two truths, one nebula. Science begins where single answers end.
level_87_after.text_zh: 两个窗口，两个真相，一片星云。单一答案结束的地方，科学才开始。

level_86_before.speaker: Maya
level_86_before.text_en: The Orion Nebula, one last time. Tonight you choose the observatory. Ask yourself first: WHAT do I want to know? The glowing gas? The hidden newborn stars? Each instrument answers a different question.
level_86_before.text_zh: 最后一次猎户座大星云。今晚由你来选观测台。先问自己：我想知道什么？发光的气体？隐藏的新生恒星？每台设备回答的是不同的问题。

level_86_before.speaker: Maya
level_86_before.text_en: There is no single 'best telescope'. There is only the right instrument for the question.
level_86_before.text_zh: 不存在唯一"最好的望远镜"，只有最适合这个问题的仪器。

level_86_after.speaker: Maya
level_86_after.text_en: You didn't just pick a device — you matched an instrument to a question. That's what a real observatory director does every night.
level_86_after.text_zh: 你不只是选了一台设备——你为一个问题匹配了一台仪器。这正是真正的天文台台长每天晚上做的事。

level_85_before.speaker: Maya
level_85_before.text_en: FAST final: show me a complete, defensible radio detection.
level_85_before.text_zh: FAST 结业考：给我一次完整的、经得起质疑的射电探测。

level_85_after.speaker: Maya
level_85_after.text_en: Visible, infrared, radio — you can now observe in three windows of the electromagnetic spectrum. One question remains: what happens when we point ALL of them at the same target?
level_85_after.text_zh: 可见光、红外、无线电——你现在能在电磁波谱的三个窗口观测了。只剩一个问题：如果把它们全部对准同一个目标，会发生什么？

level_84_before.speaker: Maya
level_84_before.text_en: Full radio session, solo: tune, localize, measure, record.
level_84_before.text_zh: 完整射电观测，独立完成：调谐、定位、测量、记录。

level_84_after.speaker: Maya
level_84_after.text_en: From eyepieces to data traces - and you read both fluently now.
level_84_after.text_zh: 从目镜到数据轨迹——现在两种语言你都读得流利。

level_83_before.speaker: Maya
level_83_before.text_en: Interference is our light pollution: phones, satellites, engines. Learn the pulsar's voice among them.
level_83_before.text_zh: 干扰就是我们的光污染：手机、卫星、发动机。在其中认出脉冲星的声音。

level_83_after.speaker: Maya
level_83_after.text_en: Nature repeats precisely; humans repeat messily. Now you can hear the difference.
level_83_after.text_zh: 自然的重复精确，人造的重复杂乱。现在你听得出区别了。

level_82_before.speaker: Maya
level_82_before.text_en: The feed cabin hangs from six cables above the dish. Position it finely against a turning sky.
level_82_before.text_zh: 馈源舱靠六根缆索悬在反射面上方。在转动的天空下精细地调整它。

level_82_after.speaker: Maya
level_82_after.text_en: A 30-ton cabin, placed with millimeter care. Engineering is astronomy's other half.
level_82_after.text_zh: 三十吨的馈源舱，毫米级的安放。工程是天文学的另一半。

level_81_before.speaker: Maya
level_81_before.text_en: Let the trace accumulate. In radio astronomy, time literally adds up to certainty.
level_81_before.text_zh: 让轨迹慢慢累积。在射电天文里，时间真的会累加成确定性。

level_81_after.speaker: Maya
level_81_after.text_en: The longer you listened, the clearer the heartbeat. Patience is an instrument.
level_81_after.text_zh: 听得越久，心跳越清晰。耐心本身就是一台仪器。

level_80_before.speaker: Maya
level_80_before.text_en: Tonight the pulse barely clears the noise floor. Finer tuning, slower reading.
level_80_before.text_zh: 今晚脉冲勉强高出噪声底。调得更细，读得更慢。

level_80_after.speaker: Maya
level_80_after.text_zh: 微弱的信号教出扎实的习惯。

level_79_before.speaker: Maya
level_79_before.text_en: Final radio exercise: localize the source. A radio telescope 'points' by moving its feed cabin and reading signal strength. Find where the pulse is strongest — that's your pulsar's position.
level_79_before.text_zh: 最后一次射电练习：给源定位。射电望远镜靠移动馈源舱、读取信号强度来"指向"。找到脉冲最强的位置——那就是脉冲星的方位。

level_79_before.speaker: Maya
level_79_before.text_en: No stars to hop between this time. The signal meter is your finder scope.
level_79_before.text_zh: 这次没有可以跳转的星点。信号强度表就是你的寻星镜。

level_79_after.speaker: Maya
level_79_after.text_en: You pointed a telescope with a strength meter instead of your eyes. Welcome to radio astronomy.
level_79_after.text_zh: 你用信号强度表代替眼睛给望远镜指了向。欢迎来到射电天文学。

level_78_before.speaker: Maya
level_78_before.text_en: Pulsar B0329+54: a neutron star spinning at 1.4 revolutions per second. On your trace it appears as a repeating pulse. Tune the band, watch the peaks, measure the period.
level_78_before.text_zh: 脉冲星 B0329+54：一颗每秒自转 1.4 圈的中子星。在你的信号轨迹上，它表现为重复出现的脉冲。调谐频段，观察峰值，测量周期。

level_78_before.speaker: Maya
level_78_before.text_en: There is no image here. The DATA is the observation.
level_78_before.text_zh: 这里没有图像。数据本身就是观测结果。

level_78_after.speaker: Maya
level_78_after.text_en: A rhythm from a city-sized star three thousand light-years away. When Jocelyn Bell first saw this pattern in 1967, her team jokingly labeled it LGM-1: 'Little Green Men'.
level_78_after.text_zh: 来自三千光年外一颗城市大小恒星的节拍。1967 年乔斯林·贝尔第一次看到这种信号时，团队开玩笑地把它标记为 LGM-1——"小绿人一号"。

level_77_before.speaker: Maya
level_77_before.text_en: Sweep the band slowly. Somewhere in the noise, a dead star is knocking.
level_77_before.text_zh: 慢慢扫过频段。噪声深处，一颗死去的恒星正在敲门。

level_77_after.speaker: Maya
level_77_after.text_en: There it is - the knock. 1.4 times per second, for millions of years.
level_77_after.text_zh: 找到了——那声敲门。每秒 1.4 次，敲了几百万年。

level_76_before_assembly.speaker: Maya
level_76_before_assembly.text_en: This is FAST — the Five-hundred-meter Aperture Spherical Telescope in Guizhou, China. The largest single dish on Earth. And it has NO eyepiece. Nothing to look through at all.
level_76_before_assembly.text_zh: 这是 FAST——位于中国贵州的五百米口径球面射电望远镜，地球上最大的单口径天线。它没有目镜，完全没有可以"看"的地方。

level_76_before_assembly.speaker: Maya
level_76_before_assembly.text_en: Radio waves from space are collected by the dish, focused onto the feed cabin hanging above it, and turned into electrical signals a processor can record.
level_76_before_assembly.text_zh: 来自太空的无线电波被反射面收集，聚焦到悬吊在上方的馈源舱，再转换成信号处理器可以记录的电信号。

level_76_before_assembly.speaker: Maya
level_76_before_assembly.text_en: Set up the link: active dish, feed cabin, signal processor. Forget focusing — from now on you TUNE, you don't focus.
level_76_before_assembly.text_zh: 把链路搭起来：主动反射面、馈源舱、信号处理器。忘掉调焦吧——从现在起，你要做的是调谐，不是对焦。

level_76_after.speaker: Maya
level_76_after.text_en: The link is live. Somewhere out there, a dead star is sweeping a radio beam across the Earth like a lighthouse. Tomorrow, we catch it.
level_76_after.text_zh: 链路开通了。在遥远的某处，一颗死去的恒星正像灯塔一样，用无线电波束扫过地球。明天，我们去抓住它。

level_75_before.speaker: Maya
level_75_before.text_en: Last infrared night. After this, we listen to the sky instead of looking at it.
level_75_before.text_zh: 最后一个红外之夜。在这之后，我们不再"看"天空，而是"听"它。

level_75_after.speaker: Maya
level_75_after.text_en: Cold instruments, clean signal. You now understand light our eyes can't see. But there's radiation even 'colder' than infrared — radio waves. And for those, we have a giant.
level_75_after.text_zh: 低温仪器，干净信号。你现在理解了眼睛看不见的光。但还有比红外"更冷"的辐射——无线电波。为了它们，我们有一个巨人。

level_74_before.speaker: Maya
level_74_before.text_en: Run tonight's infrared session yourself: deploy, cool, point, observe. I am only watching.
level_74_before.text_zh: 今晚的红外观测全由你来：部署、降温、指向、观测。我只旁观。

level_74_after.speaker: Maya
level_74_after.text_en: Flawless. You operate a space telescope the way you once operated a 60mm refractor.
level_74_after.text_zh: 无可挑剔。你操作空间望远镜,就像当年操作 60mm 折射镜一样自然。

level_73_before.speaker: Maya
level_73_before.text_en: Betelgeuse is cool, red, and enormous - a natural infrared beacon. Measure the giant.
level_73_before.text_zh: 参宿四又冷又红又巨大——天生的红外灯塔。测量这颗巨星。

level_73_after.speaker: Maya
level_73_after.text_en: A star too cool to dazzle your eyes, blazing in the infrared. Temperature is a point of view.
level_73_after.text_zh: 一颗冷得晃不了你眼睛的恒星,在红外里却光芒万丈。温度是一种视角。

level_72_before.speaker: Maya
level_72_before.text_en: Pull out your Newtonian records of Orion. Compare them with tonight and name what the atmosphere stole.
level_72_before.text_zh: 翻出你牛顿镜的猎户座记录。和今晚对比,说出大气偷走了什么。

level_72_after.speaker: Maya
level_72_after.text_en: Now you know exactly what leaving the atmosphere buys. It is written in your own two records.
level_72_after.text_zh: 现在你确切知道离开大气层买到了什么。答案写在你自己的两份记录里。

level_71_before.speaker: Maya
level_71_before.text_en: Map Andromeda's warm dust lanes tonight - structure no ground telescope of ours can touch.
level_71_before.text_zh: 今晚描绘仙女座温暖的尘埃带——我们任何地面望远镜都碰不到的结构。

level_71_after.speaker: Maya
level_71_after.text_en: A galaxy's skeleton, drawn in heat. Add it to the three portraits you are collecting.
level_71_after.text_zh: 用热画出的星系骨架。把它加进你正在收集的肖像里。

level_70_before.speaker: Maya
level_70_before.text_en: Today's lesson is the sunshield itself. Point the model so the shield blocks the Sun, Earth and Moon all at once. If the instruments warm up even slightly, their own heat drowns the signal.
level_70_before.text_zh: 今天的课程是遮阳板本身。调整姿态，让遮阳板同时挡住太阳、地球和月亮。哪怕仪器只是稍微变热，它们自身的热辐射就会淹没信号。

level_70_after.speaker: Maya
level_70_after.text_en: Pointing AND shading at once - now you think like a spacecraft operator.
level_70_after.text_zh: 指向和遮挡同时成立——你已经开始像航天器操作员一样思考了。

level_69_before.speaker: Maya
level_69_before.text_en: An infrared telescope that isn't cold is just an expensive heater looking at itself.
level_69_before.text_zh: 一台不够冷的红外望远镜，只是一台看着自己发呆的昂贵加热器。

level_69_after.speaker: Maya
level_69_after.text_en: Cold instruments, clean signal. Heat management IS observation in the infrared.
level_69_after.text_zh: 低温仪器，干净信号。在红外世界里，热管理本身就是观测。

level_68_before.speaker: Maya
level_68_before.text_en: The Orion Nebula again — but in infrared this time. Visible light shows you the glowing gas. Infrared passes THROUGH the dust and shows you the newborn stars hiding inside.
level_68_before.text_zh: 又是猎户座大星云——但这次用红外线看。可见光给你看发光的气体，红外线则能穿透尘埃，让你看到藏在里面的新生恒星。

level_68_after.speaker: Maya
level_68_after.text_en: Stars that no eyepiece on Earth could ever show you — because their light never reaches the ground as visible light at all.
level_68_after.text_zh: 这些恒星是地球上任何目镜都永远看不到的——因为它们的光根本不会以可见光的形式到达地面。

level_67_before.speaker: Maya
level_67_before.text_en: You're not magnifying harder. You're asking a different question: not 'what shines?' but 'what is warm?'
level_67_before.text_zh: 你不是在放大得更狠，而是在问一个不同的问题：不是"什么在发光"，而是"什么是温热的"。

level_67_after.speaker: Maya
level_67_after.text_en: You asked 'what is warm' and the sky answered differently. Keep both answers.
level_67_after.text_zh: 你问了"什么是温热的"，天空给出了不同的回答。两个答案都留着。

level_66_before_assembly.speaker: Maya
level_66_before_assembly.text_en: This is a model of a segmented space telescope — like the James Webb. Its primary mirror is 18 hexagonal segments that unfold in space, because no rocket can carry it in one piece.
level_66_before_assembly.text_zh: 这是一台分段式空间望远镜的模型——就像詹姆斯·韦布。它的主镜由 18 块六边形镜片组成，在太空中展开，因为没有火箭能整块运上去。

level_66_before_assembly.speaker: Maya
level_66_before_assembly.text_en: It does not observe visible light. It sees INFRARED — heat radiation. That's why it carries a sunshield the size of a tennis court: the telescope itself must stay colder than its targets.
level_66_before_assembly.text_zh: 它观测的不是可见光，而是红外线——热辐射。这就是为什么它带着一面网球场大小的遮阳板：望远镜自身必须比它的目标更冷。

level_66_before_assembly.speaker: Maya
level_66_before_assembly.text_en: Assemble the segments, the instrument package, and the sunshield. Every piece here answers the question: how do you see heat without feeling your own?
level_66_before_assembly.text_zh: 把镜片阵列、仪器包和遮阳板装起来。这里的每个部件都在回答同一个问题：如何看见热，而不被自己的热干扰？

level_66_after.speaker: Maya
level_66_after.text_en: Segments aligned, sunshield deployed. In space this alignment took engineers three months. You get the idea in one evening — not bad.
level_66_after.text_zh: 镜片对齐，遮阳板展开。在太空中，工程师花了三个月做这次对齐。你一个晚上就理解了它的原理——不错。

level_65_before.speaker: Maya
level_65_before.text_en: Last night with the mirror family: Mars, with the Gregorian. Newtonian, Cassegrain, Gregorian — each fold has a reason. Tonight, prove you can drive any of them.
level_65_before.text_zh: 镜面家族的最后一晚：用格里高利式看火星。牛顿式、卡塞格林式、格里高利式——每一种折叠都有它的理由。今晚，证明你能驾驭它们中的任何一台。

level_65_after.speaker: Maya
level_65_after.text_en: You've mastered every ground-based mirror we own. But everything we did tonight fought the same enemy: the atmosphere itself. Turbulence, clouds, glow.
level_65_after.text_zh: 我们拥有的每一台地面反射镜你都掌握了。但今晚我们做的一切都在对抗同一个敌人：大气本身。湍流、云层、天光。

level_65_after.speaker: Maya
level_65_after.text_en: What if the telescope simply... left the atmosphere behind? Come back tomorrow. I have something special to show you.
level_65_after.text_zh: 如果望远镜干脆……离开大气层呢？明天再来。我有特别的东西给你看。

level_64_before.speaker: Maya
level_64_before.text_en: Golden Arcturus, and a review: Newtonian, Cassegrain, Gregorian. Tell me why each fold exists.
level_64_before.text_zh: 金色的大角星，加一场复习：牛顿、卡塞格林、格里高利。告诉我每种折叠为什么存在。

level_64_after.speaker: Maya
level_64_after.text_en: Three folds, three reasons, one observer who understands all of them.
level_64_after.text_zh: 三种折叠、三个理由、一个全都理解的观测者。

level_63_before.speaker: Maya
level_63_before.text_en: Poor seeing on the strictest instrument we own. Learn which errors are yours and which belong to the sky.
level_63_before.text_zh: 最严苛的仪器遇上糟糕的视宁度。分清哪些误差是你的，哪些属于天空。

level_63_after.speaker: Maya
level_63_after.text_en: Tonight you separated instrument error from atmosphere. Few observers ever learn the difference.
level_63_after.text_zh: 今晚你分清了仪器误差和大气误差。很多观测者一辈子都没学会。

level_62_before.speaker: Maya
level_62_before.text_en: Drift correction through a folded path now. Keep Sirius centered.
level_62_before.text_zh: 现在在折叠光路里做漂移修正。让天狼星保持居中。

level_62_after.speaker: Maya
level_62_after.text_en: Steady through the fold. Your corrections are getting invisible - that is the goal.
level_62_after.text_zh: 折叠光路中依然稳定。你的修正正在变得不可察觉——这正是目标。

level_61_before.speaker: Maya
level_61_before.text_en: Can a strict two-mirror fold handle a faint nebula? Tonight we find out.
level_61_before.text_zh: 严苛的双镜折叠能应付暗弱星云吗？今晚见分晓。

level_61_after.speaker: Maya
level_61_after.text_en: It held. Precision and light-gathering are not enemies - just an expensive friendship.
level_61_after.text_zh: 它扛住了。精密和集光并不敌对——只是这份友谊比较昂贵。

level_60_before.speaker: Maya
level_60_before.text_en: Study Jupiter's cloud bands tonight. Contrast - not size - is what this design defends.
level_60_before.text_zh: 今晚研究木星云带。这个设计守护的不是大小，是对比度。

level_60_after.speaker: Maya
level_60_after.text_en: The bands separated cleanly. Contrast is a quality you feel before you can measure it.
level_60_after.text_zh: 云带分得干干净净。对比度是一种先感觉到、后测量到的品质。

level_59_before.speaker: Maya
level_59_before.text_en: Mars tolerates no sloppiness through this instrument. Check your alignment before you blame the sky.
level_59_before.text_zh: 这台仪器上,火星不容忍任何马虎。怪天空之前先检查你的对准。

level_59_after.speaker: Maya
level_59_after.text_en: Strict optics teach honest observers. You are becoming one.
level_59_after.text_zh: 严苛的光学教出诚实的观测者。你正在成为其中之一。

level_58_before.speaker: Maya
level_58_before.text_en: Vega one more time. Upright, high-contrast, fringe-free - the Gregorian's quiet signature.
level_58_before.text_zh: 再看一次织女星。正立、高对比、无彩边——这是格里高利镜低调的签名。

level_58_after.speaker: Maya
level_58_after.text_en: Three telescopes have now shown you the same star three ways. Keep all three records.
level_58_after.text_zh: 三台望远镜已经用三种方式给你看了同一颗星。三份记录都留好。

level_57_before.speaker: Maya
level_57_before.text_en: Sirius again — you observed it with the refractor long ago. Same star, different physics. Pay attention to where the focus 'snaps in' compared to what you remember.
level_57_before.text_zh: 又是天狼星——很久以前你用折射镜看过它。同一颗星，不同的物理。注意它"咔哒"合焦的位置和你记忆中的差别。

level_57_after.speaker: Maya
level_57_after.text_en: A tighter, cleaner point than the refractor ever gave you — and no purple halo. Focus position is a property of the OPTICS, not just of your fingers.
level_57_after.text_zh: 比折射镜给过你的更紧致、更干净的星点——而且没有紫色光晕。合焦位置是光学系统的属性，不只是你手指的功劳。

level_56_before_assembly.speaker: Maya
level_56_before_assembly.text_en: The Gregorian uses a CONCAVE secondary mirror placed BEYOND the primary's focus. The light comes to a focus, spreads out again, and only then hits the secondary.
level_56_before_assembly.text_zh: 格里高利式使用一面凹面副镜，放在主镜焦点的更远处。光先聚成一个焦点，再散开，然后才碰到副镜。

level_56_before_assembly.speaker: Maya
level_56_before_assembly.text_en: That extra focus point makes the image upright — sailors loved it centuries ago — but it demands stricter alignment: two curved mirrors AND a mid-air focal point must all agree.
level_56_before_assembly.text_zh: 多出来的这个焦点让成像是正立的——几百年前的航海家很喜欢这一点——但它对光轴要求更严格：两面曲面镜和一个悬在半空的焦点必须完全一致。

level_56_before_assembly.speaker: Maya
level_56_before_assembly.text_en: This is not 'a fancier telescope'. It's a different geometry with its own price. Assemble it and feel the difference.
level_56_before_assembly.text_zh: 这不是"更高级的望远镜"，而是一种有自己代价的不同几何。装好它，体会那种差别。

level_56_after.speaker: Maya
level_56_after.text_en: Two curved mirrors, one mid-air focus, zero fringe. Feel how much stricter the alignment wanted to be.
level_56_after.text_zh: 两面曲面镜、一个悬空焦点、零彩边。感受一下它的对准要求严格了多少。

level_55_before.speaker: Maya
level_55_before.text_en: Cassegrain final: folded optics under real conditions. Everything counts tonight.
level_55_before.text_zh: 卡塞格林结业考：真实条件下的折叠光学。今晚每一项都算分。

level_55_after.speaker: Maya
level_55_after.text_en: You've now used mirrors two ways: straight through (Newtonian) and folded back (Cassegrain). There's a third, stranger fold — the Gregorian. It's older than both.
level_55_after.text_zh: 你已经用过两种镜面布局：直通的牛顿式和折返的卡塞格林式。还有第三种更奇特的折叠——格里高利式。它比前两种都古老。

level_54_before.speaker: Maya
level_54_before.text_en: Clouds cross a narrow field fast and completely. Time your observation between them.
level_54_before.text_zh: 云穿过窄视场又快又彻底。在云缝之间安排观测。

level_54_after.speaker: Maya
level_54_after.text_en: Narrow field, narrow windows. You used both well.
level_54_after.text_zh: 窄视场,窄窗口。你两个都用好了。

level_53_before.speaker: Maya
level_53_before.text_en: Same Jupiter, two mirrors. Compare your Newtonian log with tonight - honestly.
level_53_before.text_zh: 同一颗木星，两种镜子。把牛顿镜的记录和今晚的对比——要诚实。

level_53_after.speaker: Maya
level_53_after.text_en: Wide and bright versus large and detailed. Neither wins everywhere - instruments are choices, not upgrades.
level_53_after.text_zh: 宽而亮对阵大而细。没有全场获胜者——仪器是选择，不是升级。

level_52_before.speaker: Maya
level_52_before.text_en: Turn all that focal length on the Moon. Single craters will fill your eyepiece.
level_52_before.text_zh: 把这一整段焦距对准月球。单个环形山就能填满你的目镜。

level_52_after.speaker: Maya
level_52_after.text_en: You walked the rim of a single crater tonight. That is what folded focal length buys.
level_52_after.text_zh: 今晚你沿着一座环形山的边缘走了一圈。这就是折叠焦距买来的东西。

level_51_before.speaker: Maya
level_51_before.text_en: At this magnification the sky RACES across the field. Keep Mars centered - corrections small and quick.
level_51_before.text_zh: 在这个倍率下，天空在视场里飞奔。让火星保持居中——修正要小要快。

level_51_after.speaker: Maya
level_51_after.text_en: Drift scales with power. You just proved it with your own hands.
level_51_after.text_zh: 漂移随倍率放大。你刚用自己的手证明了这一点。

level_50_before.speaker: Maya
level_50_before.text_en: High power is a deal with the atmosphere. Tonight the air is against you - negotiate.
level_50_before.text_zh: 高倍率是和大气做的交易。今晚大气不配合——去谈判吧。

level_50_after.speaker: Maya
level_50_after.text_en: You waited for the still moments and they came. High power rewards patience, not force.
level_50_after.text_zh: 你等到了平静的瞬间。高倍率回报的是耐心，不是蛮力。

level_49_before.speaker: Maya
level_49_before.text_en: Mars is small and low tonight — the harshest test for a folded telescope. Use everything you know: careful finding, patient focus, waiting out the turbulence.
level_49_before.text_zh: 今晚的火星又小又低——这是对折叠望远镜最苛刻的考验。用上你会的一切：仔细寻星、耐心调焦、等待大气平静。

level_49_after.speaker: Maya
level_49_after.text_en: Small disk, long focus, honest result. Log what the air allowed.
level_49_after.text_zh: 小圆面、长焦距、诚实的结果。把大气允许你看到的记下来。

level_48_before.speaker: Maya
level_48_before.text_en: A narrow field punishes sloppy aiming. Tonight the finder scope earns its keep again.
level_48_before.text_zh: 窄视场惩罚粗糙的瞄准。今晚寻星镜又要立功了。

level_48_after.speaker: Maya
level_48_after.text_en: Found it clean. Old skills never expire - they just meet harder telescopes.
level_48_after.text_zh: 干净利落地找到了。老本事不会过期——它们只是遇到了更难的望远镜。

level_47_before.speaker: Maya
level_47_before.text_en: Jupiter under long focal length: the disk will look bigger than you've ever seen it. But the narrow field means careful finding, and the seeing will shimmer at high power.
level_47_before.text_zh: 长焦距下的木星：圆面会比你以往见过的都大。但窄视场意味着寻星要更仔细，而且高倍率下大气视宁度会让它微微晃动。

level_47_before.speaker: Maya
level_47_before.text_en: If the image boils, don't fight it — wait for the still moments.
level_47_before.text_zh: 如果图像在"沸腾"，别硬扛——等那些短暂的稳定瞬间。

level_47_after.speaker: Maya
level_47_after.text_en: Cloud bands at high power — worth every bit of careful aiming. High magnification is a deal with the atmosphere: it shows more only when the air allows.
level_47_after.text_zh: 高倍率下的云带——所有仔细的瞄准都值了。高倍率是和大气做的交易：只有空气允许时，它才让你看到更多。

level_46_before_assembly.speaker: Maya
level_46_before_assembly.text_en: A Cassegrain hides a long telescope inside a short tube. The primary mirror sends light to a convex secondary, which bounces it BACK through a hole in the primary.
level_46_before_assembly.text_zh: 卡塞格林把一台长望远镜藏进短镜筒里。主镜把光送到一面凸面副镜上，副镜再把光反射回来，穿过主镜中央的开孔。

level_46_before_assembly.speaker: Maya
level_46_before_assembly.text_en: Folding the path doubles up the focal length in a compact body. Long focal length means high magnification with the same eyepieces.
level_46_before_assembly.text_zh: 折叠光路让长焦距装进紧凑的机身。长焦距意味着同样的目镜能得到更高的倍率。

level_46_before_assembly.speaker: Maya
level_46_before_assembly.text_en: The catch: a narrow field of view, and high power magnifies every wobble and every ripple of the atmosphere. Build it, then aim carefully.
level_46_before_assembly.text_zh: 代价是：视场很窄，而且高倍率会放大每一次晃动和大气的每一丝抖动。先装好它，然后瞄准时要格外仔细。

level_46_after.speaker: Maya
level_46_after.text_en: Compact outside, long inside. Now let us see what a folded light path does to a planet.
level_46_after.text_zh: 外面紧凑，里面很长。现在看看折叠光路对行星能做什么。

level_45_before.speaker: Maya
level_45_before.text_en: Dobsonian final: big mirror, simple box, your hands. Prove the combination.
level_45_before.text_zh: 多布森结业考：大主镜、简单摇箱、你的双手。证明这个组合。

level_45_after.speaker: Maya
level_45_after.text_en: There's a trick: fold the light path. That's the Cassegrain, and it's next.
level_45_after.text_zh: 有一个妙招：把光路折叠起来。那就是卡塞格林——我们的下一台望远镜。

level_44_before.speaker: Maya
